what_it_does: Declares a structured decision record named reconnect-tools about authorizing a one-time reconnect that switches on waiting tools, offering a choice between reconnecting now or waiting to batch.
observations: The file contains a single module-level DECISION dictionary with id reconnect-tools.
It defines two options: Reconnect now and Wait and batch it, with the latter marked recommended: true.
It includes a legibility block with name, is, and why fields.
It has scope global and subtype authorize.
There are no functions, classes, or other code symbols.
standouts: The decision is framed as a one-time action only Tim can perform.
The recommended option is to wait and batch rather than reconnect immediately.
Provenance is explicitly anchored to an external markdown line reference.
conventions: Uses a module-level DECISION constant for structured decision metadata.
Separates machine fields (id, options, scope, subtype) from human-readable legibility text.
References an external source with a code:// URI and line anchor.
concerns: The claim that only Tim can perform the action is stated but not enforced or explained in this file.
The recommended flag is set without any visible justification or criteria.
The provenance comment mentions verbatim-verified by recollection, which is not independently verifiable here.
notes: A decision ledger entry asking whether to reconnect now to activate waiting tools or batch the reconnect later.
questions: Who is Tim and how is the exclusivity of this action enforced?
What specific tools are waiting and what does reconnect mean technically?
What criteria make the wait-and-batch option recommended?
What happens if the reconnect is never performed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a declarative decision record about a one-time reconnect authorization, with no implementation logic.
apparent_themes: Decision-as-code and design substrate captured as data.
Trade-off between immediate activation and batching for fewer interruptions.
Provenance and legibility for decision rationale.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to capture a pending authorization choice in a structured, readable form so the authorized person can decide when to trigger a one-time reconnect.
novelty: It treats an operational timing decision (when to reconnect) as a versioned code artifact with options, a recommendation, and provenance.
contribution: A structured decision record that preserves the question, options, recommendation, rationale, and provenance for a one-time reconnect authorization; useful for decision logs and audit trails.
summary_for_embedding: A decision ledger file defining the reconnect-tools authorization with options to reconnect now or batch later, including legibility text and a provenance link.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate a pending one-time reconnect decision and guide the authorized person toward the recommended wait-and-batch option.
